reviews.title: Not a Five Stars Hotel, for sure...
reviews.text:
Plays as a 5 stars Palace "Online" but far from it.
The staff don't speak English or so little.
The 5 meters deep sand beach is dirty, I don't tell you about the water that is even worst.
Only one restaurant and once is enough to discover the "Chef" talent and the napkins are like Cardboard Packaging.
AirCon is noisy and when you switch it off you hear the next bungalow's one. When all AC are off you hear the Jacuzzi filter pump... No free internet in the room and no phone signal neither but they list the bungalow for 500€/night.
My bungalow 502 was close-by the parking lot with traffic, so... NO WAY to relax in the garden or in the Jacuzzi at sunset time.
Pattaya is one hour round trip and 1,000thb from hotel.
If this is five stars, how many stars should get the Ritz Paris or the Pierre NYC ?
I believe the main mistake comes from a very poor management. The land and the garden are precious and it could be a fantastic resort hotel with a good management but... Far from it today.
reviews.positives: I am wondering about the Pro... Maybe the swimming pool.

reviews.title: Great resort
reviews.text:
It's the 3rd time staying at Sea Sand Sun Resort, and during my last stay (June, 2012) it rained a bit and there were literally hundreds of bugs in my room. Instead of changing the room for us, the resort had a staff come and sweep the bugs. Obviously, there were still 20-30 bugs still in the room after the job was done. We had to ask for a vacum cleaner because they refused to change the room for us. Apparantly it was our fault that the bugs got into the room; I guess the cracks under the doors are my responsibility.  In the end, they cleared all the bugs and the room was perfect once again, though we wasted over an hour before it was totally clean.  My previous encounter with this resort was fabulous so I shall not criticize too much.  Unless you have your own car, the location of this resort is not very convenient to do anything.

It's too bad I have to give poor rating this time for the bug problems.

reviews.title: A weekend of weddingd
reviews.text:
Rooms are gorgeous, well maintained, well appointed and spacious. The gardens too are beautiful and very well kept.
I went for a weekend from Bangkok, to relax on the beach and have a nice spa, neither of which I did. The beach has sun loungers but no umbrellas and so no shade, impossible to sit there without burning. The spa was lovely however there was a wedding going on and so the noise overruled a peaceful setting.
I know weddings are essential for hotels, however in a small resort like this they fully encroach on the regular guest experience. Example, umbrellas taken from pool for use at dining tables, cannot sit at pool. Lunch wedding buffet in restaurant so cannot dine there and no other restaurant option. Set up of loud speakers for evening wedding at 6pm, set up laying music for 4 hours before so loud. Sunbathing by the pool 3 meters away from 100+ dining wedding guests. As a result I left the hotel early. Nice place to stay but stay midweek when maybe no weddings.
reviews.positives: Location, rooms, gardens

reviews.title: Nice comfy private room
reviews.text:
The resort in general is very nice, with lot of nature and very private rooms. It is very quiet, and the pool & ocean area is really relaxing. You feel you are in the South of Thailand, not in Pattaya. 
However it is 30 minutes away from Center of Pattaya, so it is not recommended if you do not have a car, even if I understood the hotel has shuttle service (not free!)
The breakfast is basic, but very ok, even though they do not change so for a long stay the breakfast would be a bit boring. 
Also, the TV in the bedroom was bad located, so we could not watch the TV in the bed... bad design!
The bathroom was amazing, spacious and relaxing. 
Overall it is a good stay, relaxing compared to hotels in center of Pattaya. Staff is very nice. We had a good stay.
reviews.positives: Large room, nice bathroom, natural surrounding, isolated rooms

reviews.title: A perennial favourite in Phatthaya
reviews.text: This is my second time to stay at this resort, the first time leaving a good impression largely untainted by the second.  This resort has the most beautiful landscaping I've seen in a resort, lending a feeling of serenity and beauty to a rather large resort complex.  This time however the staff seemed on the whole to be a bit worn out, detracting somewhat from the goodness supplied by the visceral experience.  The resort features a good restaurant with live music at night played by talented musicians, but unfortunately at a volume shattering the intimacy of an otherwise highly pleasurable dinner for two by the seaside.  There is a lovely "infinity" swimming pool, and the resort also features an exercise room, billiards table and ping pong table, along with the obligatory 'spa' offering Thai massage, etc.  Our room was nicely decorated and quite comfortable.  Those fond of night life will consider the resort's location inconvenient, but those who detest the kind of night life that Phatthaya is famous for will consider it a tranquil retreat.  I will return to this resort in the future.
reviews.positives: Beautiful landscaping, quiet beach, nice room, good food
reviews.negatives: Restaurant a bit expensive and too loud at night; staff could should a little more warmth and enthusiasm

reviews.title: Not professional on the basics!!!
reviews.text:
Checking in and checking out nightmare. 

We checked in at 8PM (booked a week in advance). They couldn’t find our booking at first and took them 30mins to get us the room. Once arrived at the room, the room wasn’t lit up and Aircon wasn’t on we couldn’t went to bed until the temperature has picked up. 

Checking out
We asked them in advance that we want express checkout since we leave early in the morning at 6.30AM. Requested a baggage pickup at the time. 

No one pickup our baggages and I need to ring for a pickup and surely they went and check minibar (we have 2,000 baht deposit with them which they could deduct from it and proceed express checkout)

It was quite a shock for such a nice hotel but couldn’t handle the basics!!!

reviews.title: Nice resort hotel, excellent for family with kid
reviews.text:
The entrance is very nice, down hill road.  Hotel is very quiet, not so crowd.
I went with my family, we booked 2 rooms. 

The front reception was unfriendly service.  I drove my car to the front of hotel,nobody
pay any attention to our luggages.  I asked one male staff to have porter to unload luggage but the answer was " you drive your car near to your room, it would be more convenient for self loading " 

???????

Then I turned to another woman receiptionist, she tried to ask that staff to load for us
but he seemed unpleasant to do.

Fortunately, one golf car was coming, the driver came and very friendly to pick our luggage
he is very nice and show service mind.  

Overall hotel is very nice but quite inconvenient to walk from your room to any place in this hotel because it is large area and quite complicate.

Foods is simple, we took dinner in the only restaurant in this hotel, it is next from the beach.  It is very nice.  Foods is simple, taste is somewhat better than other hotels we ever taken.  Price is very expensive for simple foods ( Ladna, Pad Thai Kung, )
We took one dish for each, total 6 persons, we paid 2,600 Baht ??

Staff in restuarant has very good service mind, she pay a very good attention to our family

Swimming pool is close to the beach and very nice, we can enjoy sea view during swiming in the pool.
reviews.positives: Hotel room, surrounding garden and swimming pool next to beach

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text:
- ห้องพักใหญ่และกว้างดี แต่เตียงเล็กไปหน่อย (ห้องใหญ่แต่ให้เตียงขนาดควีนไซส์) เมื่อเทียบกับราคาห้องแล้วคิดว่าไม่เหมาะสมเท่าไหร่
- อาหารเช้าบุฟเฟ่ต์ มีให้เลือกไม่ค่อยเยอะมาก แต่อาหารรสชาติอร่อย มีน้ำผักผลไม้สกัดเย็นให้ทานด้วย แต่สลัดบาร์ไม่มี
- สระว่ายน้ำ กระเบื้องยาแนวไม่มี ทำให้มีความคมมาก เวลาจับหรือเดินในสระ กลัวโดนบาดมากๆ
- สปา -- ไม่ได้ใช้บริการ
- ที่จอดรถ มีสองจุด หนึ่งจุดบริเวณล็อบบี้ อีกหนึ่งจุดบริเวณหน้าห้องอาหาร มีรถกอล์ฟคอยให้บริการในบริเวณที่พัก
- บริเวณที่พักเป็นเขาๆ นิดหน่อย สามารถเดินได้ มีถนนให้เดินสะอาดและกว้างดี เดินรอบหนึ่งก็เกือบๆ 1 กิโลเมตรอยู่
- ชายหาด ติดทะเลเลย เป็นหาดส่วนตัว แต่ดูจากน้ำทะเลแล้วคิดว่าลึกมาก น่ากลัว ไม่กล้าเล่น หาดไม่กว้างมาก ทรายนิ่มดี
- มีโต๊ะปิงปอง กับโต๊ะพูลให้เล่น ฟิตเนสก็มีอุปกรณ์หลากหลายดี แต่มีลู่วิ่งอันเดียว ถ้าจะไปเล่นกับแฟน ต้องบริหารดูดีๆ
- สตาฟช่วงเช้าวันเช็คเอาท์ที่ห้องอาหาร น่ารักและเป็นกันเองมาก ประทับใจสตาฟ ให้ความช่วยเหลือดีมาก
- ไม่ค่อยประทับใจที่ฟรอนท์ ไม่มีเจ้าหน้าที่คอยประจำอยู่ที่หน้าโต๊ะเช็คอิน เหมือนเจ้าหน้าที่ไปหลบอยู่ที่อื่น ต้องรอสักพักถึงจะเดินออกมากัน ทั้งตอนเช็คอินและเช็คเอาท์เลย

reviews.title: Everything as expected
reviews.text:
Everything was pretty much as we expected as we had done some research on the resort and the general area beforehand. The standout feature is the resort's beautiful garden and general surrounds which are very well sculpted and maintained. The semi-private beach is reasonably clean and secluded, and thankfully free of the army of hawkers and umbrellas of other beaches. The sea isn't exactly crystal clear but it's better than many other more popular beaches in the region.  The pool area is right next to the beach and is quite spectacular, but there weren't enough deckchairs for everyone so we mostly lay on the grass next to the beach. There is a themed water park nearby if the kids get bored. 
Our room was good, although we learned later that we had been upgraded. They pushed the twin beds together for us, making more than enough room for our young daughter to share. The breakfast was good, but quite expensive if you paid separately (we paid around $25 per breakfast upfront at check-in for our daughter, which wasn't refundable if she chose not to use it). We mostly ate elsewhere during our stay but other guests said the restaurant was good for lunch and dinner, but pricey.
Be warned that the resort is in a quiet area and far from the main tourist town of Pattaya, which is either a good or bad thing depending on your perspective (a good thing for us!). It's also several hundred metres from the main road so taxis aren't the most convenient option for getting out and about. We had our own car so I can't comment on the hotel's own options for day-trips and tours. 
All-in-all this resort is best for those who want to chill out and do nothing in a secluded beachfront venue for a few days.
reviews.positives: Beautiful gardens; secluded and reasonably clean beach; far from the madness of Pattaya

reviews.title: Good place for weekend get away
reviews.text:
Overall speaking it's a great place to go and re-visit at Pattaya area (actually it's another 30 mins drive from centre of Pattaya). The resort itself are all good, worth a bit more than 4 stars. You won't be disappointed for your valuable time and budget to be there. Just a few things to bear in mind: 

1.The location is remote from popular area. If you get there without owned vehicle, according to the resort staff, one trip to the centre Pattaya by taxi will be 550 Baht, round trio will be 1100 Baht. Just go to some 7-11 nearby the resort will be 350 Baht for one trip by taxi, and 700 for round trip, which is a bit pricy for me. Surely you can take an 10 mins walk out of the resort and waiting for the local "pick-up community truck". but I suggest go with some snacks and beverages in your luggage. 

2. The restaurant "Horizon Sea View Restaurant" is fine. The ABF is worth to wake up earlier. Even though everyone has different taste, I can say here the ingredient they pick for the food are pretty good.   

3. For your budget, book during weekday and, if possible, choose Ocean Front Pool Villa Suite or Pool Villa Suite and spend 2 nights there is really recommended! If go with kids, no worried, they will be very well treated!
reviews.positives: Tranquil and friendly place for weekend relaxing. Private beach is a big plus! The swimming pool with multi depth also nice and safe for kids under 12.

The Hotel itself is one of the best I've stayed in of its type. The location is great, only 20mins away from the hustle and bustle of central Pattaya and you would never know it.

reviews.title: Good hotel with friendly staff
reviews.text: The room is cheaper than any five star hotel in Central Pattaya as Holiday Inn or Sheraton but we got a larger room, fully equipped facilities ( my favorite is an espresso machine from Lavazza! You can imagine what I feel, my girls are happy with fully equipped rest room, they didn't need to ask for anything more.) with our own outdoor jacuzzi and patio. The surrounding is better, good privacy beach for watching sunset from there or a pool next to the beach. An included breakfast is plenty of food ( not much as Sheraton but it's ok for a small hotel like this, the staff will be there when you need anything.
reviews.positives: Privacy, large room, fully equipped

reviews.title: pleasantly surprised! very happy with this place..
reviews.text:
nice rooms. far from filth of pattaya. well maintained beach. nice designed rooms. plenty of trees and good landscaping. restaurant with view of ocean. peaceful place.
we had a big problem with air con in our basic room the first night. went to complain to mgmt, fearing standard indifference, but were very surprised with competent management. they apologized and moved us to another room, upgraded for our troubles.
we actually returned once again since our first visit, and we liked the place just as much.
minor point: the swimming pool could use a minor rennovation, but its nothing to put you off this overall very good place. will go back soon.
reviews.positives: competent management, clean beach, natural setting.

reviews.text:
if you want to escape the crowd in pattaya, this will be an excellent choice. relax decoration, private cottage house, friendly staffs (not over-polite that make you nervous), "free" breakfast in five stars level. although the pool is not really big, it is enough for young couples to spend their time in lovely sunbath. 
the only drawback is the beach...... although it is so called private but you just cannot swim with it poor water condition.....not clear and with lots of sting under the sea. 
however i think this is the problem of whole pattaya rather than the resort itself. 
so if you really prefer water sports, go to other place of thailand!

reviews.title: Hotels that offer floating breakfast.
reviews.text:
The hotel is located next to the beach, where you can enjoy the sunset sea view from the pool. They offer a buffet breakfast with good variety and quality. The restaurant is right next to the pool, providing excellent views, and you can also pay extra for a floating breakfast (serving 2-4 people at THB 1500).

On the day of check-in, it was my wife's birthday, and the hotel provided decorations and a mini birthday cake, which was very thoughtful.

The private pool attached to the room is clean, and flower petals are placed on the ground to enhance the garden's beauty. However, the transparent glass next to the toilet and bathtub in the room has no curtains. Even though there are trees planted outside and a concrete wall, it feels a bit insecure. It would be better if curtains could be added so that guests can choose whether to use them.

reviews.title: High Quality Resort
reviews.text: In general I’m not someone that likes to stay in resorts, with their connotations of being a world away from the rest of the world. This resort was the location of a family wedding and it worked brilliantly for that. The landscaping and gardens are very beautiful, all set on a lovely shell shaped beach. You are a long way however from local shopping and everyday facilities, so come prepared. This accommodation was lovely, especially the outdoor shower, but was Sea, Sand and Sun was much more expensive than other places that we stayed in Thailand, and its not  easy to eat anywhere other than the resort’s restaurant. The breakfasts were included in our deal and they were magnificent.

reviews.title: Recommended for those who are not fond of the hustle and bustle of Pattaya!
reviews.text:
【Location and Facilities】
It was a very quiet and relaxing resort located about 30-40 minutes from Pattaya city, including the beach. The grounds were full of tropical trees, making me feel like I was always healing while walking in a large park.
【Breakfast】
The breakfast was very delicious, mainly featuring Thai cuisine, and included an egg and noodle station and a fresh juice bar, offering a wide variety that was very satisfying. I stayed for two nights, and the fact that the Thai menu was completely different each day was a high point. It was a bit disappointing that the desserts were only pastries. The breakfast restaurant was hot, with only fans.
【Room】
During my visit in May, there were many humid days, but the air conditioning worked well, making it comfortable. The Deluxe Pavilion room I stayed in was spacious enough for three people with the extra bed. The amenities were minimal, with only shampoo and body soap provided in the shower room, and no conditioner. As is common in Thailand, the tap water had an unpleasant smell, which was slightly uncomfortable even when using it for showers or brushing teeth.
【Pool】
The pool had a children's area and a jacuzzi area. Towels were available for rent. There were about 12 reclining chairs by the poolside. Since there weren't many guests, there were available seats. The water temperature was warm, allowing for prolonged enjoyment.
【Service】
There was a day when the room wasn't cleaned, but when I requested it at the reception, they quickly provided towel and drinking water replacements. The middle-aged female manager present at the breakfast area was always smiling and engaging in conversation, and she was very kind.
【Impression】
I liked it so much that I want to plan a trip to Thailand just to come back to this hotel! I especially loved the spaciousness and design of the room, as well as the diverse and delicious breakfast!

It was attached to another room with a door separating the two rooms. I could literally hear EVERYTHING that was going on in the room next to me. The occupants were not being loud by any means but I could still hear everything they were saying and doing. I am a light sleeper so this was a problem for me. When I asked to be moved to a standalone room I was told there were none available. I would definitely recommend this resort to others but I would suggest asking for a standalone room unless this is not an issue for you.

reviews.title: Nice boutique hotel near Pattaya
reviews.text:
We had a one night's stay here over the new year. I booked the Garden Villa and was upgraded to Garden Pavillion, which is bigger and nicely decorated.

The hotel grounds were very well maintained. Esp. love their infinity pool, facing the sea. Water is a bit cold when I am there but we still had good fun in the pool. The beach in front of the infinity pool is ok, clean and not much people are there. So you could have a quiet sunbath unlike in Pattaya Beach.

Breakfast choices were excellent, they even had French cheese!

The transfer from hotel to airport charged by the hotel is exorbitant (B4500). Well but you do have other choices, a chartered taxi would cost around B1,200

Overall an excellent stay and we wish we could  stayed longer!oh and the welcome drink is soooo delicious...!

reviews.title: An excellent property on the beach
reviews.text:
We loved this property! From check in to check out we had a fabulous experience. The property is located away from the hustle-bustle of the Pattaya city on the peaceful Jomtein beach.  It is like a little paradise, covered with beautiful trees with nature sounds throughout the day. If you love the peace and quiet you will also love this place. 

The breakfast spread is impressive and the food never disappoints. The staff offers cold beverages as well and we had the lovely Thai tea everyday during our stay. For dinners we used Grab food delivery which was very convenient and cost effective. For deliveries you need to go to the lobby as they do not allow outsiders to enter the resort. The buggy service is just a phone call away so it is no hassle.

The pool is fairly large with a separate kids pool and a Jacuzzi, so don’t forget to pack your swimwear. 

The highlight for us was the spa which was extraordinary. We experienced the Thai massage and the oil massage. The masseuses are very skilled and courteous. Do book in advance as they are quite busy. 

The only opportunity is with the gym where the equipment is fairly old and some of it has rust on it. Though it’s still functional and has two very effective AC units.

The pool table is in good shape but I wish there was air conditioning in this spot as there is no ventilation in the basement. 

Overall we loved our stay thanks to the friendly staff and amazing Thai hospitality. I would highly recommend this hotel.

reviews.title: Fantastic Stay
reviews.text: We stayed there for 7 nights in a Pool Villa. Very nice and spacious room with sliding door access to the private pool and an extra access via the lockable semi outdoor shower doors. The breakfast buffet has a selection of both Asian and western cuisine. The staff are absolutely respectful and kind. A big mention to Suwannee S. the PR & CS Manager who always had a smile on her face and a warm greeting. She always checked to make sure everything was ok with our stay and offered any help if we needed. The Horizon restaurant offers great service and food. The beach is clean but the water in the Gulf of Thailand is not as clear like you would find on the Thai islands or the Andaman sea. The infinity pool is sufficient for a swim but the beach is only a few metres away. The resort has beautiful and well maintained gardens. The access through the resort at night could be better lit along the walkways. There are many squirrels you can see during the day running along the trees or top of roofs and frogs in the evening. The walk to Bang Sare is easy if you walk along the beach to the road, then it’s about a 20 minute walk to the Bang Sare seafood markets and restaurants. Plenty to choose from. You will find 7-11, local traders and other stores to shop in. If you want to drive there, it’s about 10 minutes and you can book a car through Grab or Bolt and it will cost you under 80 baht one way. We used both but found Bolt was better than Grab. There is also Aquaverse by Colombia Pictures which is a water park and great for kids just up the road. We travelled to Jomtien beach which is about 30 minutes and 160 baht one way. It was easy to get a Bolt back to the resort considering its location. After visiting Pattaya central, I’m glad we chose Sea Sand Sun resort and villa for our stay. It’s a more refined area without the hustle and bustle of Pattaya. People say it was remote but we didn’t find that. We never had a problem catching a ride share to anywhere and therefore travelled to various locations. Overall, a great stay and great staff. Definitely recommend this for a quiet get away.

reviews.title: Resort that will come again.
reviews.text: We spent a beautiful vacation here. The hotel room is spacious, the mattress is of medium firmness, and we slept very well. The hotel is surrounded by lush greenery, making it feel like living in a forest, and it has direct access to the beach, with soft and fine sand. You can swim in the hotel pool or in the sea, although the pool tiles are a bit rough on the feet. The hotel restaurant has an incredibly beautiful view, and the atmosphere for breakfast, afternoon tea, and dinner is excellent. If within budget, it is a super suitable hotel for family relaxation and vacation.

reviews.text: I decided to take a break from  Bangkok at very last minute and wanted to stay on the beach.  This hotel is away from Patong and you would need a car to get around. reviews.title: a quiet and comfortable place to rest
reviews.text: Located in Pattaya, Thailand, this wonderful resort is surrounded by a quiet beachfront rainforest far from the bustling heart of Pattaya. Mornings in this peaceful place are harmonized by the sounds of birds in the forest, streams, and the leaves of palm trees blown by the wind. It has provided me with time for peace of mind and calm meditation. It must be the best place for travelers who want to take a break from their routine. Also, the dedication of the staff to providing the best spa and massage services, excellent restaurants with an exotic atmosphere, and friendly and caring service was very impressive. The main pool was also modest in size, and visitors could enjoy leisure activities such as kayaking at the beach close to the resort. For travelers who want to relax, the resort must be a place that offers a very perfect environment.

reviews.title: Upgrade and birthday cake
reviews.text: This is my third stay. It has been over ten years since my last stay, and I was worried about the facilities being outdated, but they were renovated, and it was very nice. When I mentioned that it was my birthday during the reservation, I received a room upgrade and a birthday cake, which made me very happy. Thank you very much. The room service was delicious, and there were many options for breakfast, allowing me to relax comfortably within the hotel. I really like the layout of the room. It has a sense of heaviness and makes me feel rich.

reviews.title: A relaxing trip with mom~
reviews.text:
This was my first time in Southeast Asia, and I traveled with my mom, who had surgery earlier this year. We stayed 4 nights in Bangkok and 1 night in Pattaya before crossing over. We had to drive 30 minutes south from downtown Pattaya, but thanks to the location, we were able to relax and enjoy the end of our trip properly^^

✅️ Friendliness
The taxi driver dropped us off not at the lobby but in the parking lot where the restaurant is located. A staff member in the restaurant contacted the lobby for us, and we were taken to the lobby in a buggy while they explained the resort to us. I trusted my credit card at check-in, but American Express didn’t work (make sure to bring Visa or MasterCard)ㅠㅠㅠ I didn’t have enough cash for the deposit - there were no ATMs in the resort and no convenience stores around, so I couldn’t withdraw money - but the staff said it was okayㅠㅠ and let it go;;;; Thank you!

✅️ Facilities and Cleanliness
The resort is so beautiful, it's pure nature. I stayed in a pool villa that was clean and surrounded by many plants, which I loved :) Before coming here, I stayed at three hotels and resorts, and the towels here were the whitest and thickest.

✅️ Breakfast Restaurant
During the week I spent in Thailand, staying at two hotels and two resorts with my mom, we tried all the breakfasts, and this place is the best!!! My mom also acknowledged, 'This is a breakfast hotspot,' and even as someone who usually just lightly enjoys drinks and salads, I found the pho and desserts incredibly tasty^^ I plan to come back here just for the breakfast next time.

✅️ Disappointing Point
While using the pool, sink, and shower, I strongly sensed a unique bleach? chemical product? smell in the waterㅠㅠ I didn’t use it separately, but the small pool next to the restaurant is cozy.

reviews.title: Paradise on earth
reviews.text: This hotel is situated in what must be the closest to the garden of Eden that you can get on this earth. It is simply stunning. The pool is beautiful and clean; the rooms, even the ones at the 'budget' end of the scale, are gorgeous: dark wood, glass wall, beautiful bathrooms, soft linen, clean, simple, and good, effective air con. The breakfasts are outstanding. There is anything you might want, from Eastern food - noodles, rice, soup - to Western standards, such as bacon, egg, sausage, toast, and continental pastries. The restaurant is open-sided and looks out to sea. It really is the most breath-taking hotel. The only annoyance was that people did tend to rush out to the pool and sea at what must have been dawn, to 'bag' a sun-bed. The staff really do need to address this practice and prohibit it, since there are not quite enough sunbeds to go round, and if you have paid top-dollar to stay here, you really do want to be guaranteed a sunbed. Besides that, though, it is a paradise.

reviews.text: I'm impressed with everything, especially all the staff who provided excellent and polite service. We requested a birthday bed decoration, and the hotel arranged it beautifully. In the evening, while having dinner at the hotel, the staff surprised us with a small cake and there was a live singer who sang birthday songs, which was incredibly sweet. The breakfast buffet had a wide variety of delicious food and even cold-pressed juices. We could eat to our heart's content and loved that we could order unlimited cold drinks as well. The hotel is spacious, peaceful, and very private, with a lush environment that feels like being in a big jungle. However, this means there are some insects, earthworms, millipedes, and snails around, especially since we stayed in the garden villa, so we had to be careful not to step on any. There are golf carts available for transport. This is the resort that impressed me the most, and I will definitely return. 💖💖💖

My daughter enjoyed her breakfast so much. I had spend a lot of time at the pool. Staffs were friendly and helpful. I would recommend this resort to whoever need a real holiday in Pattaya.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: Очень понравилось местоположение, номер, шикарная территория. Быстрая доставка в номер еды. Персонал шустрый и отзывчивый.
reviews.negatives:
1. Повар в ресторане не умеет абсолютно готовить морепродукты. Из морепродуктов очень скудное меню, только ассорти из морепродуктов доступно к заказу, но из этого ассорти можно съесть только кальмара и креветок ботанов. Не смотря на то,что в соседней деревне Бан-Сарае морской порт и всегда есть свежайшая рыба в неограниченных количествах, отель не закупает у них морепродукты, очень обидно. Почему нельзя готовить кальмаров, макрель, баракуду, групперов, тунца. Салат с тунцом и тот с консервированным. Позор для Тая. 
2. Нет спасателя ни на пляже, ни на бассейне. Ребята из персонала в ресторане разрываются. Они же и смотрят за лежаками, раздают полотенца, таскают лежаки и зонты и тут же принимают заказы в ресторане и выносят еду. Благо мусор на пляже убирает специальный человек. Хотя делает это рано утром, а в течении дня всего пару раз. Туристы сами выкидывают постоянно мусор из воды, как то стремно для 5 звезд.
3. Оказывается одноразовые вафельные тапочки у них под отчет, т.е. они их стирают и выдают заново. Т.е. при выезде, если вы их выкинули или случайно оставили на пляже, с вас взыщут 20 баксов за пару.
4. Ужасный Wi-Fi, он постоянно отваливается либо вообще не пропускает интернет. В итоге сидел на 5g на местной сим-карте.

reviews.title: Everything is good, worth choosing here.
reviews.text: A shady place with clean beaches, lots of big trees, quiet and peaceful, perfect for relaxation. The rooms are spacious and clean, with complete amenities. All the staff are lovely. The television is a smart TV, connected to the internet. There is a variety of breakfast options, with fresh cold-pressed juices to choose from, and coffee and tea can be ordered as freshly brewed. I really liked it, nothing to criticize at all. I will definitely come back next time.

reviews.title: This place is perfect for any party and any occasion. A paradise for singles,couples, families, and special events
reviews.text:
This was an absolute turnaround experience for visiting pattaya, most of the villas just have a nice home and a beach, but these villas are accompanied by some ancient and majestic gardens full of the most tranquilizing sounds. I entered complete serenity on the porch of my deluxe villa, and enjoyed an absolute lovely evening, morning, and afternoon, night, etc. for the two days I was there.

The service was excellent. The food was delicious and Michelin star (I recommend the pad Thai with tiger prawn) and I also had the salmon which was relishing with anything on their menu.

The 🪴 garden was absolutely breathtaking. I mean really, jaw droppingly outstanding. Some of these trees were just so unique and large that they must have been there from before Jesus was born (seriously) and then they up kept the perfectly overlain overgrowth & largely massive gardens from sunrise to sunset. I read that it kept guests there, but for us, it’s what attracted us there and the entire experience kept us there.

This place is almost a solid 9/10 and I never ever ever give 9/10 ratings. 4.5/5.

reviews.title: idyllic retreat
reviews.text:
this is the 5th time I have stayed at this resort, for a getaway break in a truly relaxing and calming environment it is hard to beat.
The pool villas are great, what I love is the setting within a well maintained and beautifully presented tropical garden. It is the garden and trees along with the waterfalls and fountains that just make it so incredibly relaxing.
The staff are great, always very helpful and consistently go above and beyond what would be normally expected.
I have been very happy to see that a place I think I wonderful are also constantly looking to make improvements.
great stay and o look forward to returning again
